title: Federal Reserve cuts interest rates by 0.5%
summary: The Federal Reserve announced a surprise rate cut of 0.5% to support economic growth. The move is expected to boost borrowing and investment across sectors.

title: Apple reports record quarterly earnings, beats expectations
summary: Apple Inc. announced Q4 earnings of $1.50 per share, exceeding analyst estimates of $1.42. Revenue grew 8% year-over-year driven by strong iPhone sales.

title: US GDP growth accelerates to 3.2% in Q4
summary: The US economy grew at an annualized rate of 3.2% in the fourth quarter, up from 2.8% in Q3. Strong consumer spending and business investment drove the expansion.

title: Inflation surges to 7.5%, highest in 40 years
summary: Consumer price index rose 7.5% year-over-year in January, the highest reading since 1982. Energy and food prices contributed significantly to the increase.

title: Major tech company announces 10,000 job cuts
summary: A leading technology firm revealed plans to lay off 10,000 employees, representing 5% of its workforce, citing economic uncertainty and cost pressures.

title: Economists warn of potential recession in H2
summary: Leading economists predict a 60% probability of recession in the second half of 2024, citing inverted yield curve and weakening manufacturing data.

title: Federal Reserve schedules policy meeting for March
summary: The Federal Open Market Committee announced its next scheduled policy meeting will be held March 19-20, 2024, following the regular calendar.

title: Tesla releases Q4 delivery numbers
summary: Tesla Inc. reported vehicle deliveries of 484,507 units in Q4 2024, in line with analyst consensus estimates of 483,000 units.

title: Commerce Department releases monthly retail sales data
summary: The US Commerce Department is scheduled to release December retail sales figures on Thursday at 8:30 AM ET, providing insights into consumer spending trends.

title: SEC announces routine review of quarterly filings
summary: The Securities and Exchange Commission published its standard schedule for reviewing corporate 10-Q filings for the current quarter, affecting approximately 500 publicly traded companies.
